name: Aryaman
description: Aryaman is a significant, though often understated, Aditya in the Hindu pantheon. As an Aditya, he is one of the sons of Aditi, the mother of the gods, and embodies a specific aspect of the solar principle. Aryaman is primarily associated with the concepts of contracts, oaths, and the proper conduct of societal norms. He presides over the bonds of friendship, marriage, and agreements, ensuring that these commitments are honored and upheld. Unlike some of the more prominently featured Adityas like Varuna or Mitra, Aryaman's role is more subtle, focusing on the maintenance of social order and the integrity of relationships. His influence is pervasive in rituals and ceremonies related to marriage and agreements, where his presence is invoked to guarantee fidelity and commitment. Although less frequently celebrated through independent hymns, Aryaman's importance is underscored by his consistent appearance alongside other Adityas, reinforcing his essential role in the cosmic and social order. He represents the binding forces that hold society together, emphasizing the importance of ethical behavior and the sanctity of promises.
subtitle: Guardian of Contracts

keyMyths.title: Aryaman and the Wedding Vows
keyMyths.description: In many Hindu wedding ceremonies, Aryaman is invoked to witness and sanctify the vows exchanged between the bride and groom. The belief is that Aryaman ensures the couple remains true to their promises of love, fidelity, and mutual respect throughout their married life. He acts as a divine guarantor, holding them accountable to the commitments they make before the sacred fire. This invocation underscores the importance of marital fidelity within Hindu tradition and the role of divine forces in upholding societal norms.
keyMyths.source: Kane, P.V. History of Dharmaśāstra. Bhandarkar Oriental Research Institute, 1941.

keyMyths.title: Aryaman as Witness to Agreements
keyMyths.description: Aryaman is often called upon to witness agreements and treaties between individuals or groups. When oaths are sworn in his name, they carry immense weight, as breaking such an oath is believed to invoke his divine wrath. This myth highlights the cultural importance of honesty and integrity in all dealings, with Aryaman serving as a constant reminder of the consequences of dishonesty and broken promises. His presence ensures that individuals act responsibly and honorably in their interactions.
keyMyths.source: Macdonell, Arthur Anthony. Vedic Mythology. Strassburg: K.J. Trübner, 1897.

keyMyths.title: The Adityas' Shared Responsibility
keyMyths.description: While Aryaman has specific domains, the Adityas as a group work together to uphold cosmic order. One myth describes how the Adityas, including Aryaman, collectively battle forces of chaos and darkness. Aryaman's contribution to this battle lies in ensuring that agreements among the gods are honored, preventing internal strife and maintaining a unified front against their adversaries. This story demonstrates that Aryaman's role is vital for the stability of the entire cosmos.
keyMyths.source: Bergaigne, Abel. Vedic Religion. Motilal Banarsidass, 1978.

sources.author: Macdonell, Arthur Anthony
